description: In Rust, the toolchain channel (rust-toolchain.toml), edition, and enabled features are part of the contract and changing them changes behavior.
body: In Rust, the toolchain channel (rust-toolchain.toml), edition, and enabled features define how code compiles, lints, and resolves dependencies; bumping any of them can change diagnostics and emitted output. Use when you touch the toolchain channel (rust-toolchain.toml), edition, and enabled features or when a build behaves differently than the pinned environment. Do not silently upgrade the toolchain or override the pinned version to make an error disappear. Actions: read the pinned config, match the project's the toolchain channel (rust-toolchain.toml), edition, and enabled features, then run cargo clippy -D warnings and rustc lint output and cargo build (and cargo build --release) with the workspace's feature flags to compare against the baseline. Validation signals include identical cargo clippy -D warnings and rustc lint output output, a reproducible build, and unchanged lockfile resolution. Risk boundary: a toolchain bump is a deliberate, reviewed change; escalate rather than upgrading to unblock yourself.
